text.content:
Between adventures, your hero can pursue personal goals — forge a legendary weapon, uncover forbidden lore, build a stronghold, or cure a companion's curse. These are downtime projects, and they happen during respites.

How Projects Work

A downtime project is a long-term task you work on across one or more respites. Each respite, you can spend your respite activity making a project roll to earn project points toward a project goal. When your accumulated points meet or exceed the goal, the project is complete.

Key Concepts:

  • Project Goal: The total number of project points needed to finish the project.
  • Project Roll: A special test — no tiers. Whatever you roll is the number of points you earn (minimum 1).
  • Breakthrough: A natural 19 or 20 on a project roll. You earn your points and immediately make another project roll as part of the same respite activity.

Starting a Project

Before you can begin, you need two things:

Prerequisite What It Is
Item Prerequisite A special item, component, or material needed to start the project. You can't begin without it. Some projects have none.
Project Source Lore that guides the work — a book, schematic, expert tutor, or ancient inscription. You must have access to the source whenever you make a project roll.

You can start as many projects as you like, but you can only work on one project per respite.

Making a Project Roll

Each project specifies which characteristic you roll with (Might, Reason, Intuition, etc.). Add any applicable skills — crafting skills for crafting projects, lore skills for research, or others at the Director's discretion. The total you roll is the number of project points you earn.

Edges and Banes on Project Rolls:

  • Edge: +2 to the roll (as usual).
  • Double Edge: +4 to the roll.
  • Bane: −2 to the roll.
  • Double Bane: −4 to the roll.

The minimum result on a project roll is always 1, even with penalties.

Language and Project Sources

Project sources are written or shared in a specific language. How well you know that language affects your work:

Your Knowledge Effect on Project Roll
Know the language No penalty.
Know a related language Project roll takes a bane.
Know neither Project roll takes a double bane.

Helping with Projects

Other heroes (or NPCs) can contribute to your project by spending their own respite activity to make a project roll on your behalf. Their points add to your project's total. A hero serving as a living project source (sharing their expertise) must also use their respite activity.

Guides

Sometimes you find a guide — a manual, schematic, or knowledgeable NPC — that gives a specific number of project points toward a project without requiring a roll. Just study the guide as your respite activity and add its knowledge value to your project's total. A guide can only be used once per project.

If the guide is in a language you know, you gain its full knowledge value. If it uses a related language, you gain only half (rounded down).

Career Project Points

Some careers grant you starting project points that represent work you did before the campaign began. You can put these points toward any project you qualify for — possibly finishing simpler projects before the first session even starts.

Career Type Starting Project Points
Artisan, Disciple, Sage 240
Criminal, Farmer, Laborer, Warden 120
Other careers 0

Types of Projects

Crafting Projects

Crafting projects let you create vehicles, supernatural treasures, teleportation platforms, and more. Other creatures can help with crafting by contributing their own project rolls.

Common Crafting Projects:

  • Craft Treasure: Create a supernatural item (weapon, armor, implement). Each treasure lists its own prerequisites, source, and goal.
  • Imbue Treasure: Enhance an existing mundane item with supernatural power. An item can be imbued up to 3 times (at 1st, 5th, and 9th level). Imbuing also grants bonus Stamina.
  • Build Airship: Goal 3,000. Requires a Wind Crystal of Quintessence.
  • Build or Repair Road: Goal = 10 × length in miles (halved for repairs). Earns Renown.
  • Craft Teleportation Platform: Goal 1,500. Requires a spatial navigator.

Research Projects

Research projects let you uncover information — trace a lost treasure, decipher a ritual, expose a conspiracy, or find a cure.

Knowledge Type Project Goal Examples
Common 15 Noble's secret courtship, an alternative name for a deity, local rumour tracing.
Obscure 45 A ritual to open a portal, the weaknesses of a specific monster, a guild's hidden charter.
Lost 120 The location of a legendary workshop, a dead language translation, an ancient battle site.
Forbidden 240 Secrets guarded by a powerful faction, encrypted texts in dead languages, divine taboos.

Find a Cure: Research a cure for a disease, curse, or supernatural affliction. The project goal equals 50 × the level of the creature that caused the affliction. Item prerequisite: remains of the creature (or one of the same kind). Once completed, you can craft additional doses for half the project points.

Tips for Players

  • Plan ahead. Keep an eye out for item prerequisites and project sources during adventures. That ancient tome or monster component might be exactly what you need.
  • Use your career points. If your career gave you starting project points, spend them — you might finish a simple project before play even begins.
  • Collaborate. Allies can spend their respite activity to make project rolls on your behalf. A party working together finishes projects much faster.
  • Learn the language. A bane or double bane on every project roll adds up. If a project source is in a language you do not know, consider finding a tutor or a translated copy.
  • Fish for breakthroughs. A natural 19 or 20 gives you a bonus roll — big projects move faster with a little luck.
  • Talk to the Director. If you want to research something specific or craft a custom item, ask. Projects are flexible by design.
